# kubeseal_sealed_secret (Resource)

Encrypts Kubernetes secret data into a SealedSecret manifest using the sealed-secrets controller certificate.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Name of the secret.
- `namespace` (String) Namespace of the secret.
- `secret_data` (Map of String, Sensitive) Map of secret key-value pairs to encrypt.

### Optional

- `annotations` (Map of String) Annotations to apply to the SealedSecret template metadata.
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels to apply to the SealedSecret template metadata.
- `scope` (String) Sealing scope: strict, namespace-wide, or cluster-wide.
- `type` (String) Kubernetes secret type (e.g., Opaque, kubernetes.io/tls).

### Read-Only

- `encrypted_data` (Map of String) Map of encrypted key-value pairs (base64-encoded ciphertext).
- `id` (String) Identifier in the format namespace/name.
- `input_hash` (String) SHA-256 hash of the inputs, used internally for plan stability.
- `sealed_secret_json` (String) The full SealedSecret manifest as JSON.
- `sealed_secret_yaml` (String) The full SealedSecret manifest as YAML.
